title: acaena caespitosa
intro: Acaena caespitosa, also known as the bluebells or woolly-leafed caterpillar plant, is a small, perennial flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Acaena caespitosa is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
